channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Iran’s authorities project hardened stance with more talks on horizon
item.description: Abbas Araghchi met senior Pakistani officials in Islamabad and left for Oman to be later bound for Russia.

item.title: Trump cancels US envoys’ trip after Iran’s Araghchi leaves Pakistan
item.description: Trump later suggests that next talks will be over phone, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!!!'

item.title: CIA agents reported killed in Mexico were not authorised to operate: Gov’t
item.description: Mexican government says it is seeking details after incident raises questions about US activities on its territory.

item.title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif meet in Pakistan
item.description: Iranian FM Araghchi met Pakistani PM Sharif in Islamabad as part of efforts to revive direct negotiations with the US.

item.title: African governments need to take urgent action on fertiliser shortages
item.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have slowed fertiliser shipments which could put harvests at risk.

item.title: Indonesian UN peacekeeper becomes sixth killed in Lebanon
item.description: An Indonesian soldier has become the sixth UN peacekeeper killed in recent hostilities in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Flights resume at Tehran airport as US-Iran ceasefire holds
item.description: International flights have resumed at Tehran’s Imam Khomeini International Airport, after weeks of disruption by the war

item.title: Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran airport
item.description: Commercial flights begin for the first time since the conflict with the US and Israel began about two months ago.

item.title: Iran’s Infowar: Lego, AI and ever tightening control
item.description: The tools and techniques behind Iran’s push in the battle of narratives.

item.title: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
item.description: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.
